content: Antibiotic resistance is spreading at an alarming rate, posing a significant threat to public health. The main driver of this trend is the overuse and misuse of antibiotics. When antibiotics are used excessively, they kill off susceptible bacteria, leaving behind resistant strains that can multiply and spread. This process is accelerated by the widespread use of antibiotics in agriculture, where they are often used to promote growth and prevent disease in livestock. As a result, resistant bacteria can enter the food chain and spread to humans. Additionally, poor hygiene and infection control practices in healthcare settings also contribute to the spread of resistant bacteria. The consequences are severe, with antibiotic-resistant infections becoming increasingly difficult and costly to treat, and in some cases, deadly. Urgent action is needed to curb the overuse of antibiotics and prevent the spread of resistant bacteria.
